channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: 22 dead amid gang clashes in Ecuador's largest city
item.description: Gang infighting has left nearly two dozen people dead in Guayaquil, Ecuador's largest city. 14 people were arrested in the aftermath of the Thursday skirmish.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clashes in Syria between government forces and Assad loyalists kill more than 200
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Fighters siding with Syria's new government stormed several villages near the country's coast, killing dozens of men in response to recent attacks on government security forces by loyalists of ousted President Bashar Assad, a war monitor said.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cyclone Alfred weakens as it closes in on Australia's east coast
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Alfred was downgraded to a tropical low weather system Saturday as it closed in on Brisbane, Australia. Over 19,000 people have been evacuated, and one remains missing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In 2015, Obama committed the US to achieving UN global goals by 2030. Trump just rejected the goals
item.description: In 2015, then-President Barack Obama committed the United States to achieving newly adopted U.N. global goals by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukraine supporters focus on hope and resilience as US relations sour and war carries on
item.description: Religious leaders, scholars and diplomats have gathered at the University of Notre Dame in Indiana for a conference on Ukraine studies with a theme of hope.
item.dc:creator: Peter Smith, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Polish leader Tusk plans large-scale military training for all adult males to boost reserves
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk said Friday that his government is working on a plan to prepare large-scale military training for every adult male in response to the changing security situation in Europe.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 3 Bulgarians convicted of spying for Russia across Europe from base in UK
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Three Bulgarian nationals based in Britain were convicted Friday by a London jury of spying for Russia on what police said was “an industrial scale.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Far-right front-runner in Romania’s annulled presidential race files his candidacy in the rerun
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romanian far-right populist Calin Georgescu filed his candidacy Friday in a rerun of last year's presidential election that was annulled by a top court after his surprise first-round win.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Canadian foreign minister: Trump tariffs 'pretext' to annexation
item.description: Canadian Foreign Minister Mélanie Joly on Thursday claimed that President Trump's tariffs against Canada — the U.S.'s largest trading partner — are just a "pretext" for the administration's wish to eventually annex the nation.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 90,000 Palestinians attend the first Friday prayers of Ramadan in Jerusalem
item.description: Some 90,000 Palestinians have prayed at the al-Aqsa mosque compound in Jerusalem’s Old City under tight security by Israeli forces.
item.dc:creator: Imad Isseid And Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Unexploded WWII-era bomb causes travel chaos in Paris, disrupts Europe's busiest railway hub
item.description: The discovery of a huge unexploded World War II-era bomb caused transportation chaos in Paris on Friday, including the suspension of high-speed train links with London and Brussels and the closure of a vital road artery into the French capital.
item.dc:creator: Samuel Petrequin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nearly 100 people died of cholera in less than a month in Sudan's White Nile State
item.description: Nearly 100 people have died of cholera in two weeks since the waterborne disease outbreak began in Sudan’s White Nile State.
item.dc:creator: Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope Francis still receiving oxygen therapy, will not read Sunday prayers: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is still receiving oxygen therapy and will not be able to read Sunday prayers, according to the Vatican. The pontiff is now entering his fourth week in Rome's Gemelli Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European leaders downplay skepticism from Trump about NATO solidarity
item.description: MADRID (AP) — European Union leaders on Friday downplayed skepticism from U.S. President Donald Trump about solidarity among NATO members a day after they backed plans to spend more on defense amid wavering U.S. support.
item.dc:creator: SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Norway's crown princess condition worsens as she battles lung disease
item.description: Norwegian Crown Princess Mette-Marit's chronic lung disease is affecting "her ability to perform her duties," the Royal House of Norway announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN says 186 missing and at least 2 dead after migrant boats capsize off Yemen and Djibouti
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — At least two people have died and 186 others are missing after four boats carrying migrants from Africa capsized overnight in waters off Yemen and Djibouti, the U.N.'s migration agency said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Court orders South Korean President Yoon released from jail for his criminal trial over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court on Friday ordered impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol to be released from jail, a move that could allow Yoon to stand trial for his rebellion charge without being physically detained. Yoon was arrested and indicted in January over the Dec.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia attacks Ukraine's energy supplies as US cuts its access to satellite images
item.description: Ukrainian officials said Russia had targeted energy facilities with dozens of missiles and drones in a nighttime attack.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A weak Pope Francis thanks people for their prayers 'from the bottom of my heart' in audio message
item.description: ROME (AP) — A weak and breathless Pope Francis thanked people for their prayers for his recovery in a remarkable audio message broadcast Thursday, the first public sign of life from the 88-year-old pope since he was hospitalized three weeks ago with double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Olympic snowboarder now on FBI's 10 most wanted list
item.description: Former Olympic snowboarder Ryan Wedding was added to the FBI’s 10 most wanted list Thursday for allegedly running a transnational drug trafficking network.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ontario will tariff electricity going to 3 US states on Monday, premier says
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said three states would face tariffs on electricity after a week of President Trump’s swipes at Canada with fluctuating trade policies. “I love Americans. It's been 20 years of my life.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine will struggle to strike targets inside Russia without US intelligence
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The U.S. decision to stop sharing military intelligence with Ukraine hobbles its ability to strike and defend against the Russian army, and increases the pressure on it to accept a peace deal being pushed by the Trump administration. Earlier in the week, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas brushes off Trump's threat and says it will only free hostages in return for a lasting truce
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas on Thursday brushed off President Donald Trump's latest threat and reiterated that it will only free the remaining Israeli hostages in exchange for a lasting ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lone wolf attacks surge in the West as terrorism intensifies globally
item.description: The Global Terrorism Index says 93% of all fatal terrorist attacks in the West are carried out by lone wolves.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tariffs not the way Trump should slow fentanyl flow: Bolton
item.description: "American importers pay the tariffs and then do everything they can to pass that cost on to American consumers," John Bolton said.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump sticking to Gaza relocation plan, as White House seems to dismiss Egyptian proposal
item.description: NSC spokesperson Brian Hughes states that Gaza is currently uninhabitable, and President Donald Trump remains committed to rebuilding Gaza free from Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump issues 'last warning' to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday issued what he called a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza, directing a sharply worded message after the White House confirmed that he had recently dispatched an envoy for unprecedented direct talks with the mi
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US aircraft carrier arrives in South Korea after North Korea test-fired missiles
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A U.S. aircraft carrier arrived in South Korea on Sunday in a show of force, days after North Korea test-launched cruise missiles to demonstrate its counterattack capabilities.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis stable a day after respiratory crisis, spent 'long periods' off noninvasive ventilator
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in stable condition Saturday a day after a respiratory crisis during his battle with double pneumonia, the Vatican said. He spent long periods of the day off the noninvasive mechanical ventilation he initially needed in a sign that his lung function was improving.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: USAID cuts are already hitting countries around the world. Here are 20 projects that have closed
item.description: Some 10,000 contracts with the U.S. Agency for International Development were terminated on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, WILSON MCMAKIN and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, NewsNation
